title: Problem → Reaction → Solution
summary: The signature pattern Unimetrix 1 uses to explain how world events are engineered.
markdown:
**Problem → Reaction → Solution** is the signature analytical pattern Unimetrix 1 returns to
whenever it explains world events. It is the archive's shorthand for a manufactured crisis: a
**problem** is deliberately created, a public **reaction** of fear or demand follows, and the
architects then supply a pre-planned **solution** that advances their aim.

Unimetrix 1 applies the model across the collection — to [Operation Lusterkill](/lore/operation-lusterkill/)
and the pandemic, to the Beirut explosion, to geopolitical
manoeuvring, and to the technologies of control it associates with the
[Nazi faction](/lore/nazi-faction/) and [DARPA](/lore/darpa/). In its telling, the "solution"
is almost always the same: greater centralization, surveillance, and dependence.

The concept doubles as a lens for reading the whole archive. Once named, it reframes each of
Unimetrix 1's accounts of current events as an instance of the same engineered dialectic — and
sets up its consistent counter-move: knowledge, equality, and self-empowerment.
